title: BLAKE2b check before groth16 parse gives actionable error
description: load_sapling_params verifies the BLAKE2b-512 hash of each param file before calling SpendParameters::read(). A contributor may suggest removing the hash check 'since groth16 parsing will fail anyway.' Without the check, a truncated or corrupt file produces a confusing internal groth16 deserialisation error with no path or hint. With the check, the user sees 'sapling-spend.params at /path/... failed BLAKE2b-512 integrity check — delete it and re-run nie wallet init.' The hash check also prevents the ~2 minute groth16 parser from running on a file that will definitely fail, which matters for UX on first-run errors.
